The system is a scheduling system.
The system coordinates aircraft arrivals.
The system coordinates aircraft departures.
The system coordinates aircraft across two runways.
The system allocates time slots.
The system prevents conflicts between concurrent runway uses.
The system gives immediate priority to declared emergencies.
The system registers incoming flights with their estimated times.
The system registers outgoing flights with their estimated times.
The system allocates each flight the earliest available 5-minute slot on either runway at or after its estimated time.
The system enforces a 3-minute separation gap between consecutive slots on the same runway.
The system gives arrivals priority over departures when competing for the same slot.
The system detects overlapping assignments on the same runway.
The system prevents overlapping assignments on the same runway.
The system detects gap-violating assignments on the same runway.
The system prevents gap-violating assignments on the same runway.
The system allows a runway to be closed.
The system automatically reassigns future flights from a closed runway to the other runway.
The system marks flights as delayed if they cannot be placed within 60 minutes of their estimated time.
The system immediately gives a declared emergency flight the earliest possible slot.
The system re-queues displaced flights by the normal rules.
The system shows a slot timetable per runway.
The system shows delayed flights on the slot timetable.
The system shows emergency events on the slot timetable.
This system coordinates both arrivals and departures of aircraft across a pair of runways.
It allocates time intervals for runway usage and ensures no overlapping assignments occur.
Operators can register flights, view timetables, and rely on automatic conflict detection.
The scheduling system produces a conflict-free runway schedule with emergency preemption.
An airport runway coordination tool that manages takeoff and landing slots, prevents concurrent use conflicts, and handles urgent priority.
